title: limonium braunii
intro: Limonium braunii, also known as Braun's sea lavender, is a rare and endangered succulent plant native to the Canary Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Limonium braunii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditional herbal preparations of Limonium braunii include infusions and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
